name: Ginkgolides
summary: Ginkgolides are diterpene lactones in Ginkgo biloba extracts, especially relevant to platelet-activating factor antagonism.
description: Ginkgolides include ginkgolide B, a known PAF antagonist; this may reduce inflammatory and thrombotic signaling, though outcomes vary by preparation.
safetyNotes: Human hemostasis evidence is for standardized whole Ginkgo biloba leaf extracts, not isolated ginkgolides, and did not show higher bleeding risk in the analyzed trials. Isolated-compound dose-response, pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, seizure, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety remains insufficient.
dosage: standardized extract or essential-oil-free preparation when oral use is intended
typical_dosage: standardized extract or essential-oil-free preparation when oral use is intended
